label: Airbus A220 (C Series)
tagline: The Airbus A220 (ex-Bombardier CSeries) is a clean-sheet narrow-body that Delta, airBaltic, and Swiss use for regional and thin mid-range…

label: Airbus A319
tagline: The Airbus A319 is the shortest A320-family variant — common on regional European routes and North American LCC fleets.

label: Airbus A320 (all variants)
tagline: The Airbus A320 is the universal narrow-body standard — operated by almost every major airline worldwide, from low-cost to legacy.

label: Airbus A320 family (A319/A320/A321)
tagline: The A320 family covers the A318, A319, A320, and A321 — the most-flown narrow-body family in commercial aviation.

label: Airbus A321 (all variants)
tagline: The Airbus A321 (and the neo / LR / XLR variants) extends the A320 family to transatlantic range on a single-aisle airframe.

label: Airbus A330 (all variants)
tagline: The Airbus A330 is a twin-engine wide-body used on medium-to-long-haul routes worldwide.

label: Airbus A340 (all variants)
tagline: The Airbus A340 is a four-engine long-hauler nearly retired from major carriers — Lufthansa, SWISS, and a few others still operate a shri…

label: Airbus A350
tagline: The Airbus A350 XWB is the newest long-haul wide-body — used by Qatar, Singapore, Cathay, and ANA on ultra-long routes like Auckland to D…

label: Airbus A380
tagline: The Airbus A380 is the double-deck super-jumbo — Emirates, British Airways, Qantas, Singapore Airlines, and Lufthansa still fly it on sel…

label: ATR 42/72
tagline: The ATR 42/72 are Franco-Italian turboprops that dominate island-hopping, short regional, and sub-400 nautical mile markets worldwide.

label: Boeing 737 (all variants)
tagline: The Boeing 737 is the world's best-selling narrow-body — from the MAX 8 on short-haul hops to the 737-800 workhorses that still anchor mo…

label: Boeing 747
tagline: The Boeing 747 — Queen of the Skies — is being retired from passenger service worldwide but still flies select routes with Lufthansa and …

label: Boeing 757
tagline: The Boeing 757 is the transatlantic narrow-body: long legs, steep-climb performance, and the last US-built single-aisle jet for charter a…

label: Boeing 767
tagline: The Boeing 767 is a mid-size wide-body still common on transatlantic and freight routes, slowly being replaced by the 787.

label: Boeing 777 (all variants)
tagline: The Boeing 777 is the long-haul backbone of most legacy carriers — the 777-300ER carries 300+ passengers across any ocean in the world.

label: Boeing 787 Dreamliner
tagline: The Boeing 787 Dreamliner is the modern long-haul choice: lower cabin altitude, larger windows, and routes that open city pairs the 747 n…

label: Bombardier CRJ (all variants)
tagline: The Bombardier CRJ family (CRJ-200, 700, 900, 1000) serves short-haul regional routes across North America and Europe.

label: Bombardier Dash 8 / Q400
tagline: The Dash 8 (especially the Q400) is a fuel-efficient turboprop used by regional carriers on sub-1-hour hops.

label: Embraer E170/E175
tagline: The Embraer E170/E175 is the regional jet workhorse of US major carriers and European regional subsidiaries.

label: Embraer E190/E195
tagline: The Embraer E190/E195 is a larger regional or small-mainline jet used by airBaltic, KLM Cityhopper, and Helvetic on secondary routes.
